name: Markdown Help Content
summary: Rich HelpContent using markdown for formatted text, links, images, and bullet points. This is the recommended way to add links and images in forms.
whenToUse: Adding formatted instructional text with headings, bold, italic, links, or bullets
Adding clickable hyperlinks — use `[text](url)` markdown syntax
When you need more control over formatting than plain HelpContent provides

help: This is a paragraph with some **bold** text and _italic_ text.

notes: Set `options.markdown` to `true` to enable markdown rendering
The `help` array contains one paragraph per entry — each entry can use markdown syntax
Supports headings (##), bold (**), italic (_), links ([text](url)), images (![alt](url)), and bullet lists (-)
For hyperlinks use `[link text](url)` — this replaces the deprecated `variant: hyperlink`
For images use `![alt text](url)` — this replaces the deprecated `variant: img`
Extended markdown syntax (tables, footnotes) is NOT currently supported
